title: Financial Literacy Training - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search 57 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness ACCENT NETWORK IMMIGRANT AND REFUGEE ASSISTANCE PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support with resumes, job searches, credential evaluation, and digital literacy, along with career counseling and career planning guidance. Also offers financial wellness services, including workshops on saving, budgeting, and debt management, and one-on-one coaching. 12254 SW Garden Place Tigard, OR 97223 Eligibility: Immigrants, refugees, and asylum seekers with a focus on Russian and Ukrainian population Hours: Monday/Wednesday 10am-8pm Email (971) 280-6738 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ACCESS HOMEOWNERSHIP CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF HUD-certified counseling agency that provides various homebuyer education programs, housing counseling, foreclosure intervention counseling, and down payment assistance. Also provides financial education and counseling services that includes in-person class online money management courses. 3630 Aviation Way Medford, OR 97504 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-5pm, Friday 8am-4:30pm Email (541) 774-4305 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BEAVERTON RESOURCE CENTER FINANCIAL LITERACY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides one-on-one coaching in personal finance topics, including budgeting, paying off debt, and saving for emergencies. 13565 SW Walker Road Beaverton, OR 97005 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Wednesday 10am-11am Email (503) 591-9028 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BIRCH COMMUNITY SERVICES SUSTAINABLE FAMILIES PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides financial literacy courses. Program participants have access to an on site food pantry. Participating households are required to monthly complete a 2 hour volunteer shift. 17780 NE San Rafael Street Portland, OR 97230 Eligibility: Must meet all of the following requirements: -Someone in the household is working or actively looking for work -Able to volunteer and discuss finances -Someone in the household who is able to speak conversationally fluent English for program communications Hours: Monday/Wednesday/Friday 9am-6pm Email (541) 251-5431 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CASA LATINOS UNIDOS COMMUNITY BUILDING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides leadership development, education such as English as a Second Language classes (ESL), financial literacy, and civic engagement. 3460 Midvale Drive Corvallis, OR 97333 Eligibility: Members of the Latinx community Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm Email (541) 512-4949 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CASA LATINOS UNIDOS LEBANON OFFICE COMMUNITY BUILDING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides leadership development, education such as English as a Second Language classes (ESL), financial literacy, and civic engagement. 500 Weldwood Drive Building E Lebanon, OR 97355 Eligibility: Members of the Latinx community Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm Email (541) 512-4949 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CASCADIA HEALTH CASCADIA PLAZA HEALTH CENTER CASCADIA CENTRAL INTAKE AND REFERRAL LINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides an intake line for services such as case management, individual, family, group, geriatric and LGBTQ counseling, alcohol and drug treatment services, DUII and gambling addiction/treatment services, recovery housing services, sexual abuse counseling, domestic violence services, urgent mental health services, medication management, money management, outreach, crisis intervention, outpatient mental health services, and early intervention services. Provides about 1000 units in sites operating as licensed residential, permanent supported housing, and independent housing. 4212 SE Division Street Suites 100 and 200 Portland, OR 97206 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (503) 674-7777 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CLATSOP COMMUNITY COLLEGE SMALL BUSINESS DEVELOPMENT CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides confidential one-on-one business advising to new and existing businesses through personalized training and workshops. Assists with business and marketing plans, financial proposals, and provides market research assistance. 1651 Lexington Avenue Astoria, OR 97103 Eligibility: Current or prospective for-profit business owners Hours: No standard hours Email (503) 338-2402 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY ACTION WASHINGTON COUNTY BEAVERTON MULTI-SERVICE CENTER FINANCIAL EDUCATION CLASSES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a variety of financial education classes covering topics such as understanding banking services, short- and long-term financial planning and stability, building and repairing credit, and understanding the basics of retirement planning. All classes are online through Zoom. 17933 NW Evergreen Place Suite 315 Portland, OR 97229 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Visit website for current class schedule. Also provides one-on-one financial counseling and student debt counseling. 528 Cottage Street NE Suite 1C Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Varies by program Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-6pm Email (503) 779-2680 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DEVNW DEVNW SPRINGFIELD FINANCIAL WELLBEING SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides classes, workshops, and one-on-one counseling in the areas of homeownership and personal finance. NeighborWorks Homeownership Center offers HUD-certified homeownership counseling and foreclosure avoidance counseling. Also provides one-on-one financial counseling and student debt counseling. 212 Main Street Springfield, OR 97477 Eligibility: Varies by program Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-6pm Email (541) 345-7106 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DIVISION OF FINANCIAL REGULATION CONSUMER FINANCIAL SERVICES INFORMATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Consumer advocates provide information on a variety of consumer financial services issues, including: -Managing finances: Tips for managing finances, getting out of debt, and learning about state-chartered banks and credit unions -Protecting finances: Tips for avoiding investment fraud, protecting one's identity, and getting help with collections or repossessions -Loans and debt help: Information about personal loans, building credit, and resources for preventing or managing a foreclosure -Securities and investments: Information about investing and help choosing a financial professional -Student loan servicing: Student loan ombudsman receives, reviews, and attempts to resolve complaints from student loan borrowers, provides borrowers information about rights and responsibilities, and directs them to resources 350 Winter Street NE Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (888) 877-4894 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EL CENTRO NW ECONOMIC EMPOWERMENT AND TAX PREPARATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides Volunteer Income Tax Assistance (VITA) and helps with Individual Taxpayer Identification Number (ITIN). Also hosts financial literacy workshops for youth and their families to help make informed decisions, strengthen economic stability, and workshops for individuals who want to start a business or improve existing business. 300 S Roosevelt Drive Suite #5 Seaside, OR 97138 Eligibility: Latinx/Hispanic community members or Spanish language speakers Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5:30pm Email (971) 414-7270 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILY NURTURING CENTER FAMILY NURTURING CENTER JACKSON COUNTY PARENT EDUCATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a way for parents and caregivers to learn discipline strategies and stregthen parent and child interactions. Provides a variety of services for children and their caregivers, including therapeutic early childhood education, home visits, parent education, respite care, and basic needs support. 825 E Main Street Suite A Medford, OR 97504 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Varies by class Email (541) 779-5242 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILY NURTURING CENTER FAMILY NURTURING CENTER JOSEPHINE COUNTY PARENT EDUCATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a way for parents and caregivers to learn discipline strategies and stregthen parent and child interactions. Provides a variety of services for children and their caregivers, including therapeutic early childhood education, home visits, parent education, respite care, and basic needs support. 214 W 4th Street Grants Pass, OR 97526 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Varies by class Email (541) 779-5242 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILY PROMISE OF LINCOLN COUNTY COMMUNITY CLASSES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers life skills, parenting, financial literacy, nutrition, and second chance renters' classes. Classes are primarily for families utilizing shelter services, but may be opened to the general public. 5030 SE Highway 101 Lincoln City, OR 97367 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Office: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm Classes: Varies Email (541) 614-0964 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FINANCIAL BEGINNINGS FINANCIAL EDUCATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides online personal financial courses to help individuals learn to manage income, eliminate debt, build credit, grow wealth, and protect their assets. Includes five courses, each 1-2 hours long, with each session focused on a key area of personal financial management. 619 SW 11th Avenue Portland, OR 972052646 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: No standard hours Email (800) 406-1876 Get Directions Visit Website More Details GUIDANCE 2 GREATNESS BEYOND REENTRY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Assists with criminal record and eviction expungement, job development, workplace training, financial literacy, felony rights, and reentry training. May be able to provide program participants with supplies such as personal protective equipment (PPE) and a desktop computer. Eligibility: -Age 18 and older -Reentry Training for women ages 16-24 -Focus on the African American and BIPOC community Hours: Not applicable Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details HOUSING WORKS BEND SATELITE OFFICE PATHWORKS PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides one-on-one coaching aimed at increasing earned income and financial literacy to make progress toward economic independence and self-sufficiency. Goal-oriented coaching sessions may focus on employment, education, home ownership, small business development, health, etc. Also offers an excrow savings program and Individual Development Account program. 1475 NE Forbes Drive Suite 110 Bend, OR 97701 Eligibility: Must hold current Housing Choice Voucher Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 923-1018 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HOUSING WORKS PATHWORKS PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides one-on-one coaching aimed at increasing earned income and financial literacy to make progress toward economic independence and self-sufficiency. coaching services with information about identifying short and long term financial goals, set spending plans, Individual Development Accounts (IDA), and budget management. Also provides a First Time Home Buyer Program which can help answer questions about the the home buying process, first time home buyer loan programs, building credit scores, and resolving challenges that may prevent homeownership. Also provides a series of workshops around the topics of financial fitness, home buying, Ready to Rent, foreclosure prevention, and default counseling. 1445 Avalon Street Klamath Falls, OR 97603 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday/Thursday 9am-5pm Email (541) 884-0649 121 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LOVE INC OF LINN COUNTY TRANSFORMATIONAL CLASSES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers faith-based classes such as Work Life Class for job and life-skills curriculum to promote job readiness and interviewing skills, Boundaries Class for understanding how to set healthy boundaries in relationships, Faith & Finances for developing practical money management skills, and Affirming Potential that helps individuals set and achieve goals. All classes begin with a meal. 1606 Santiam Road SE Albany, OR 97321 Eligibility: Low-income households Hours: Varies Email (541) 924-5683 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LOVE INC OF TIGARD, TUALATIN, AND SHERWOOD GAP MINISTRIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides clothing, hygiene supplies, household goods, bedding/linens, over-the counter medicines and supplies, financial training, assistance with resume writing, and nutrition courses. Requests are screened and referred to outside agencies for fulfillment. Assistance is dependent upon available funding and not guaranteed. Also accepts donations of bedding/linens and personal grooming supplies. 10900 SW 121st Avenue Tigard, OR 97223 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Monday-Wednesday 9:30am-12:30pm Email (503) 443-6888 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MID ROGUE FOUNDATION MOBILE NAVIGATION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides outreach assistance with housing and job search help, identification assistance (ID and Social Security cards), replacement birth certificate help with application and fee, general mail delivery, credit report requests, and SNAP and OHP application assistance. Also helps to connect people to services for savings, spending, educational plans, and life skills. Mobile Navigators meet clients in public spaces that work best for the client. PO Box 1972 Grants Pass, OR 97528 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-4:30pm Email (541) 295-5706 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MID-COLUMBIA AND COLUMBIA GORGE HOUSING AUTHORITIES MID-COLUMBIA HOUSING RESOURCE CENTER HOUSING RESOURCE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides education, information, and referral on housing issues, along with financial assistance for homebuyers and homeowners. Services include homebuyer education classes and workshops, financial education, pre- and post-purchase counseling, foreclosure counseling, down payment assistance, renter information, and guidance on maintaining and repairing a home, including home repair loans. Classes are held monthly at Columbia Gorge Community College in Hood River and The Dalles. The program also keeps the public informed about housing trends in the Mid-Columbia region using current statistics. 500 E 2nd Street The Dalles, OR 97058 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Monday-Thursday 8:30am-noon and 1pm-4pm and by appointment Email (541) 296-3397 18 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MOTHER & CHILD EDUCATION CENTER PARENTING GROUPS AND CLASSES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides childbirth education, newborn care classes, safe sleep workshop, breastfeeding workshop/support, and other classes which vary by season. 1515 NE 41st Avenue Portland, OR 97232 Eligibility: Most classes are for parents and other caregivers of children under the age of 5 Hours: Class times vary Email (503) 249-5801 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MULTNOMAH COUNTY LIBRARY EVENTS AND CLASSES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides an online community calendar to help community members find classes and events held at local libraries. Events and classes include job seeker resources (support groups and resume workshops), computer help classes and access to computers, arts and crafts instruction, book clubs and discussion groups, chess clubs, cultural celebrations and festivals, early reading skills, teen programming, lectures and author talks, money and finances programming, performances and exhibitions, story times, and special programs for parents with children ages 0-6. Eligibility: Some classes or computer lab services may require a library card Hours: Varies by class or event Email (503) 988-5123 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NATIVE AMERICAN YOUTH AND FAMILY CENTER MICRO ENTERPRISE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides technical assistance, training in business skills, assistance writing business plans, access to financial business counseling, individual development accounts (IDAs), and other support for individuals who want to establish a small business. 5135 NE Columbia Boulevard Portland, OR 97218 Eligibility: Individuals who self-identify as tribal members Hours: No standard hours Email (503) 288-8177 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NATIVE AMERICAN YOUTH AND FAMILY CENTER HOMEOWNERSHIP PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides culturally specific homeownership coaching, education, and programming to help people buy and keep their homes, increase homeownership opportunities for Portland's Urban Native Community, and close the Native homeownership gap. Also offers financial education classes. NAYA is a HUD-approved counseling agency and an equal housing opportunity provider. 5135 NE Columbia Boulevard Portland, OR 97218 Eligibility: Unrestricted, primarily serving self-identified Native One-on-one financial coaching to set financial goals and make individualized plans to achieve those goals. Financial coaches can provide encouragement; help with the basics of personal finance; assist, develop, and maintain healthy financial habits; offer credit counseling; provide accountability tools; and connect clients with resources and experts. 1400 Queen Avenue SE Suite 201 Albany, OR 97322 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (541) 924-8440 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON STATE UNIVERSITY OSU COLLEGE OF BUSINESS CENTER FOR ADVANCING FINANCIAL EDUCATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides advice, resources, and information to navigate college and make financial decisions, including self-study modules and coursework. Child care is available if requested. 1695 Jefferson Street Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: Unrestricted but with a focus on the African-American community Hours: Varies by service Email (541) 868-6337 Get Directions Visit Website More Details POLK COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION INDIVIDUAL DEVELOPMENT ACCOUNT MATCHED SAVINGS PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers Individual Development Accounts (IDAs) for first-time homeownership and secondary education goals. 657 Main Street Dallas, OR 97338 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-noon and 1pm-5pm Email (503) 831-3173 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PORTLAND COMMUNITY COLLEGE OPPORTUNITY CENTER AT 42ND AVENUE OPPORTUNITY CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides career exploration focusing on immigrant, refugee, and non-native English speakers. Courses on financial literacy, basic computer workshops, and the Steps to Success program for Temporary Assistance for Needy Families (TANF) are available. 241 SW Edgeway Drive Beaverton, OR 97006 Eligibility: Varies by program Hours: Varies by program or class Email (971) 722-2688 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PORTLAND COMMUNITY REINVESTMENT INITIATIVES INC HOMEBUYER EDUCATION AND COUNSELING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a program designed to help first-time home buyers access and maintain stable homeownership. Services include individualized pre-purchase counseling, group home buyer education, financial fitness certification, and post-purchase support. Also includes information on financial wellness and Individual Development Accounts(IDAs). Prospective clients must agree to attend orientation sessions. 6329 NE Martin Luther King Jr Boulevard Portland, OR 97211 Eligibility: Low and moderate-income households Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Email (503) 288-2923 134 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SALEM ALLIANCE CHURCH FINANCIAL PEACE UNIVERSITY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Nine-week course in a faith-based setting that teaches people how to get control of their money, stop struggling to make ends meet, get out of debt, and manage resources. 555 Gaines Street NE Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Varies by course Email (503) 581-2129 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SPECIAL EVENTS MULTNOMAH COUNTY NATIVE AMERICAN YOUTH AND FAMILY CENTER FINANCIAL EMPOWERMENT 4 YOUTH Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a series of virtual financial empowerment classes. Topics include money culture, savings goals, credit and debt, and credit scores and reports. Participants must attend all 3 sessions to receive a certificate of completion. 5135 NE Columbia Boulevard Portland, OR 97218 Eligibility: Ages 13-17 Hours: Tuesday, April 7, 14, and 21, 2026 from 5:30pm-7pm (503) 607-8704 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SPECIAL EVENTS MULTNOMAH COUNTY NATIVE AMERICAN YOUTH AND FAMILY CENTER FINANCIAL WELLNESS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a series of virtual financial empowerment classes. Topics include money culture, savings goals, credit and debt, and credit scores and reports. Participants must attend all 3 sessions to receive a certificate of completion. 5135 NE Columbia Boulevard Portland, OR 97218 Eligibility: Age 18 and older Hours: Thursday, May 7, 14, and 21, 2026 from 5:30pm-7pm (503) 607-8704 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SPECIAL EVENTS MULTNOMAH COUNTY NATIVE AMERICAN YOUTH AND FAMILY CENTER FINANCIAL WELLNESS WORKSHOP Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a one-day, in-person financial empowerment class. Topics include money culture, savings goals, credit and debt, and credit scores and reports. Coffee, snack, and lunch provided. Participants will receive a workbook and a Certificate of Completion. 5135 NE Columbia Boulevard Portland, OR 97218 Eligibility: Age 18 and older Hours: Saturday, May 23, 2026 9am-4pm (503) 607-8704 . Get Directions Visit Website More Details SPONSORS INC ROSS SHEPARD REENTRY RESOURCE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides employment, housing and resource support to individuals with conviction histories. Services include job counseling, interview preparation support, and educational workshops on resume writing, saving and budgeting, and job/housing search strategies. Also provides assistance preparing educational and financial aid applications, housing packets/applications, and other supports to help individuals with conviction histories overcome barriers to employment and housing access. 338 Highway 99 N Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: Individuals with conviction histories Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (541) 505-5682 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ST VINCENT DE PAUL OF LANE COUNTY FINDING PATHS TO PROSPERITY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a 10-hour class that teaches money management skills through hand-on activities. 2890 Chad Drive Eugene, OR 97408 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Phone: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Classes: Hours vary. Classes are offered in fall and spring. Email (541) 972-1103 Get Directions Visit Website More Details STRENGTHENING RURAL FAMILIES FINANCIAL COACHING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Free one-on-one or virtual financial coaching to set financial goals and make individualized plans to achieve those goals. Financial coaches can provide encouragement, help with the basics of personal finance, assist in developing and maintaining healthy financial habits, provide accountability, and connect clients with resources and experts. 535 S 19th Street Room 162 Philomath, OR 97370 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-5pm Email (541) 929-2535 Get Directions Visit Website More Details VENEZUELA'S VOICE IN OREGON EDUCATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides virtual education focusing on the Hispanic/Latino community. Educational workshops include Spanish language classes, financial literacy workshops, assisting job seekers with creating resumes, and mental health workshops. Also helps families navigate the educational system and advocate for equitable access to school resources and services through culturally affirming workshops and outreach. Eligibility: -Mental health workshops: Age 18 and older who identify as immigrants or refugees -All other services: Age 18 and older who identify as Hispanic/Latino Hours: Varies by event Email Get
